answer:
We have premises:  
1. \((N \supset \sim O) \supset (O \lor Q)\)  
2. \(P \supset (N \supset \sim O)\)  
3. \(N \supset P\)  

From (3) \(N \supset P\) and (2) \(P \supset (N \supset \sim O)\), by hypothetical syllogism we get \(N \supset (N \supset \sim O)\), which simplifies to \(N \supset \sim O\). This is an immediate one-step consequence using modus ponens or hypothetical syllogism.  

Thus the correct answer is **G**.  

\boxed{G}
